attack_surface.data_description: The arbitrary nature of 'remote calls' executed via InterchainAccount Contract (318), potentially exploiting vulnerabilities in target contracts on the remote chain.

security_gap.gap_description: The ICA (318) might be able to call vulnerable functions on remote contracts, or the ICA itself might have a flaw allowing broader privileges than intended.

actor_risk.description: Deployer (106) can craft specific remote calls to exploit vulnerabilities if they exist in target contracts or the ICA mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful privilege escalation via ICA could grant unauthorized control over assets or functions on the remote chain.

assumptions: Contracts on the remote chain that the ICA (318) is authorized to interact with may contain exploitable vulnerabilities (e.g., reentrancy, unprotected admin functions).
The ICA contract (318) itself could have an undiscovered vulnerability allowing its controller (Deployer 106) to gain more privileges than designed.

mitigation.strategy: Thoroughly audit the InterchainAccount (ICA) Contract (318) and all contracts it is intended to interact with on remote chains. Apply the principle of least privilege when defining the ICA's capabilities on remote chains. Implement strict input validation for any parameters passed in remote calls if possible within the target contracts.

description: A Deployer (106) crafts malicious 'remote calls' executed via the InterchainAccount (ICA) Contract (318) to exploit a vulnerability in a target contract on the remote chain, or in the ICA mechanism itself, thereby gaining unintended elevated privileges on that remote chain.
